draft_capital_dominates.note: strongest signal; confirms the W-test that ADP is the dominant rookie predictor

position.note: rookie booms concentrate at RB/TE; rookie WRs lower-hit; rookie QBs essentially never spike (0.026)

role_volume_required.note: no role = no boom; a rookie needs real volume. Mid/high target-share cells noisy (n<30) but clearly above the no-role floor

venue.note: home edge modest; wind suppresses booms (0/8, small n)

matchup_corrected.metric_orientation: low opp_passp/opp_runp = SOFT defense (code: opp_passp<=35 is 'soft pass-D')
matchup_corrected.note: On the full sample, SOFT pass-D -> more boom (expected). The rookie-only split (n~37/cell) came out INVERTED = small-sample noise, NOT a real 'rookies boom vs tough D' effect. Treat matchup as a real but secondary modifier in the soft->boom direction.

meta.note: the model's own weekly projection barely separates rookie booms -> rookie booms are hard to call week-to-week; the predictable part is the SEASON situation (draft capital + role), not the weekly number

actionable_summary: Boom-enabling rookie situation = EARLY DRAFT CAPITAL (top-60 ADP) + a REAL ROLE (volume), concentrated at RB/TE. Matchup (soft D), home, and calm weather are secondary modifiers. Rookie WRs are lower-hit; rookie QBs almost never spike.
caveats: n=311 rookie-games / 69 rookies; games repeat per player (non-independent); single-factor splits; 'undrafted 0.155' is survivorship (undrafted rookies only appear once they earned a role).
